old_claim.note: Naval Artillery (Broadside Cannons) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Treasure-fleet defense depended on cannon-armed merchant ships and escort warships that could provide protective fire within the convoy.

source_shape.source_locator: The article says galleons could carry up to 60 cannons, merchant ships were armed with cannons, convoys had multiple warships, and one galleon could give protective fire for another.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes cannon-armed galleons, armed merchant ships, warship escorts, and protective fire within Spanish treasure-fleet convoys.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Those details make naval artillery an enabling defensive capability for the convoy system while still leaving the organizational convoy requirement separate from the weapons themselves.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: naval_artillery_broadside_cannons remains an enabling edge, now with source-locatable rationale.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to unarmed administrative sailing schedules only.
A source showed the Spanish treasure fleets operated without armed merchant ships or warship escorts.
The naval_artillery_broadside_cannons node were later narrowed to exclude shipboard cannon fire used by galleons.
short_reason: The Spanish treasure-fleet convoy system used cannon-armed ships and warship escorts.
